Bridge Walker (Level Four) — The Galliard can
create minor Moon Bridges through which he alone can
travel. This allows him to cover short distances in one
hundredth of normal travel time, even letting him vanish
from a foe's sight to immediately reappear behind the
luckless victim. These are never the safest of routes (par-
ticularly as Lunes don't guard these temporary bridges); the
denizens of the Storm Umbra sometimes follow the Galliard's
trail and spill out into the physical world. This Gift is taught
by a Lune.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point to create the
bridge. The Moon Bridge lasts for only one passage, unless the
player spends a permanent Gnosis point during its creation, in
which case it lasts until the next full moon. The maximum
distance that can be traversed by the bridge is the Garou's
Gnosis in miles.

‘w Shadows by the Fire Light (Level Four) — The Galliard
canspina tale and draw others into it, whether they're interested
ornot. This Gift is usually used at moots when telling old stories,
allowing the participants to reenact the tales themselves. How-

ever, this Gift can also be used on the unwilling, forcing

them to participate in a story of the Garou's choosing.
This Gift is taught by an Ancestor-spirit.

148 Werewolf: The Wild West


|

System: When using this Gift on the unwilling, the player
must spend a Gnosis point and score three successes on a
Manipulation + Performance roll (difficulty of the target's
Willpower). If successful, the player may tell a story and force
the victim to perform exactly as the story's character does. This
effect lasts one turn per Gnosis point spent.

‘w Heart Twister (Level Five) — The Galliard with this Gift
becomes the ultimate manipulator, capable of playing a person's
heartstrings like a fiddle just by talking to him. He could cause
someone to fall in love with him (which won'tlast, and the victim's
love may turn into justified resentment) or drive another to hate a
target of his choosing (again, this won't last, but might develop into
a full-scale feud). This Gift is taught by a Coyote-spirit.

System: The player rolls Manipulation + Empathy (diffi-
culty of the target's Willpower). Success allows the werewolf to
steer the emotions of any one individual. The more successes,
the more quickly the emotions manifest and the more strongly
theyare felt. The Storyteller should give the Galliard extra dice
for roleplaying this Gift especially well.

‘w Song of the Storm (Level Five) — The Galliardea
the very fabric of the Storm Umbra with her sor

target depend on the roll; with only one success, the canyon wal
surrounding the Black Spiral Dancers might sprout a coat of
slippery blue moss, while five successes might change all the
Dancers' weapons into silver-fanged sidewinders. The Storyteller
is encouraged to be creative with whatever change results, but
should remember that this Gift's power usually manifests in
beneficial ways — for the Moon Dancers, that is.

Ahroun

The Ahroun is born to the warrior's path, and is expected to
tear his way through anything that threatens Mother Earth.
Full Moon Gifts offer combat power and an edge in battle —
little else is necessary.

‘ Inspiration (Level One) — The Ahroun is usually the
leader of the pack in combat, and this Gift strengthens that role.
Bychannelinghis courage and resolve, the Ahroun increases the
resolve of his brethren. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point for his comrades
to automatically receive one success on any Willpower rolls made
during the scene. Note that this Gift does not affect its possessor.

‘w Razor Claws (Level One) — The werewolf may
sharpen his claws into virtual straight-razors. This
¢ Gift is taught by a Cat- or Wolf-spirit.

Chapter Six: Traits

System: The player spends a Rage point while the werewolf
takes a turn to scratch his talons over a stone or similarly hard
surface. His successive claw attacks then do an additional die
of damage for the remainder of the scene.

‘ Trick Shot (Level One) — With this Gift, the Garou
need not know the meaning of the word "impossible." Shooting
an arrow through a two-spot at one hundred paces, ricocheting
a bullet around a comer or even firing down the barrel of an
opponent's gun — the Ahroun can do them all. However, this
Gift can only indirectly do any harm to a living thing — by
shooting out a rope holding a chandelier, for instance. More
direct usage is considered unsporting by the spirits, who will not
permit such vulgarity. This Gift is taught by an Air-spirit.

System: The player adds her character's permanent rating
in Glory to her Dice Pool for the trick shot. The shot must be
declared prior to rolling, and only for a truly bizarre attempt
(simply saying "I shoot ‘im in the head!" doesn't count).

@ True Fear (Level Two) — The Ahroun can overwhelm
a particular foe with the magnitude of his power, scaring the
fool into inaction. This Gift is taught by a Fear-spirit.

System: The player rolls Strength + Intimidation (difficulty of
the target's Willpower). Each success cows the enemy forone turn;
the victim cannot attack and may try to retreat. He may defend
himself and otherwise act normally (though he will be scared).

¢# Heart of Fury (Level Three) — The Ahroun can lock away
hisangerin order tomaintain control, allowing him tofreely fightand
utilize Rage without as much chance of going into frenzy. However,
once the floodgates are opened, his anger may well overpower him as
it returns. This Gift is taught by a Bear- or Wolf-spirit.

System: The player rolls Willpower, difficulty of his own
permanent Rage. Every two successes add one to the werewolf's
frenzy difficulties for the scene. Once the scene has passed, the
Ahroun's anger returns in force; he must spend one Willpower
point or immediately make a frenzy check at regular difficulty.

‘w Sun Dance (Level Five) —
The werewolf calls on his ties to the
sun, becoming immune to all natural
forms of fire (although artificial fires like gas
fires can still harm him). Additionally, the werewolf can
ignite small parts of his body and keep them burning for
extended periods. Ahroun most commonly use this Gift to light
their manes during rituals, but they can also light claws,
fangs, arms or whatever. These flames are approxi-

mately as intense as those of a torch. This Gift is
taught by a fire elemental or Sun-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point. Artificial fires
do half damage to the Ahroun; natural (even mystical, if from
a "pure" source such as an elemental Gift) do nothing at all.
The effects last for one scene.

Tribe Gifts

Tribal Gifts are part of a great reservoir of tribal lore, main-
tained and guarded by each tribe. A Garou tends to learn tribal
Gifts from spirit servants of his tribal totem, often called that
totem's "Brood." Thesesecretsand specialties are zealously guarded
by tribal elders, and rightly seen as the heritage ofall loyal sons and
daughters of the tribe. A werewolf must be on very good terms with
a foreign tribe (or at least with a packmate of that tribe) to learn
that tribe's Gifts, and she had better be able to convince the spirit
teacher that she won't dishonor said tribe by misusing its Gifts.

Women have been mythically tied to the wilderness, and
the Black Furies are able to take strength from this bond in,
Their Gifts reflect their affinity for wild and Wyld alil

unless he spends a Gnosis point to resist the Gift's effects. Other
supernatural creaturescan spend spirit energy (Blood, Quintessence
or Glamour) to rise to their feet, but mortals remain helpless. The
Gift lasts for one turn per success, and doesn't work on spirit beings.

‘w Beast Witch (Level Five) — Like the witches of legend,
some Black Furies can assume the shapes of animals. This Gift
allows a warrior to become any normal beast from a dove to a
buffalo. The animal's powers (flight, speed, gills, etc.) come with
the form. Animal totems pass this Gift to worthy Furies, and
might revoke the favor if a wolf forgets her kinship to the Wyld.

System: The Fury spends a Gnosis point and rolls Dexterity
+ Animal Ken. The difficulty depends on the animal she
chooses; human-sized ones are easy (6), while radical changes
in size or mass are difficult (7+).

Bone Gnawers

The often underestimated Bone Gnawers have no poorer a
spiritual presence than any other tribe; in fact, they often prove
capable of getting by in places any other Garou would find
completely inhospitable. Their Gifts allow them to eke out a
meager living where no other could find food, and help them
survive the most impossible of situations.

‘w Odious Aroma (Level One) — The use of musk has long
been a defense in the wild. With this Gift, the Garou can
greatly amplify her own natural body odor, creating a debilitat-
ing stench. This Gift is taught by a Stinkbug- or Skunk-spirit.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point. For the dura-
tion of thescene, all beings within 20 feet of the Garou (provided
they possess olfactory organs) subtract one from all Dice Pools as
they choke and retch. However, the Garou subtracts two fromall
his Social Dice Pools and may be ostracized for the duration.

Stone Soup (Level One) — A Bone Gnawer with
nothing more than this Gift, a few drops of water and a small
pot can create food from even twigs and rocks. By placing
whatever smallish objects he can find in the pot and adding

opponent,

Werewolf: The Wild West

enough water to moisten them, the Gnawer creates an edible
(if rather rancid to the tongue) gruel. This Gift is taught by a
Raccoon- or Rat-spirit.

System: The Garou rolls Wits + Survival. The difficulty
depends on the objects used — 6 for twigs and deadwood, 7 for
small, harmless pebbles, and up to 10 for cyanide and the like.

* Cornered Rat (Level Two) — When a Garou is backed
into a corner with nowhere to run to, she can either give up or
fight. This Gift allows the Garou to focus her Rage and lash out
at her opponents.

System: The player spends one Rage point and rolls Strength
+ Intimidation (difficulty 8). Each success grants her an
additional die to her Brawl Dice Pools. When using this Gift,
the werewolf automatically enters into a Berserk frenzy. This
Gift lasts until combat is over or the frenzy ends.

Hide in Plain Sight (Level Two) — As the Level Two
Ragabash Gift.

whistle up wood rot of the worst kind, decaying even the stoutest

lumber ina matter of seconds. This Gift is taught by a Termite-spirit.

The glass can be removed, but this takes a full turn, and the
victim can't be running around or fighting.

4# Run for Ground (Level Four) — When scouting out the
area, a Bone Gnawer quickly picks up on the nooks and crannies
where good hiding places are available...just in case. By using this
Gift, the Gnawer can duck into a shaded area (behind a barn,
into an empty barrel) and pop out in another shady area some
distance away. This Gift is taught by a Prairie dog-spirit.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point and rolls
Manipulation + Stealth (difficulty 6). For every success scored,
the Garou may increase the distance he reappears by 20 yards —
one success and he reappears 20 yards away; 2 successes, 40

yards; etc.). He cannot reappear in plain sight — he
must emerge from another likely hiding place. *


‘@ Survivor (Level Five) — The frontier and wilderness
are sometimes more than a match for even the heartiest of
Garou — but then again, the Garou are a versatile bunch.
This Gift grants temporary immunity to many environmen-
tal effects, such as pressure or temperature variations. The
Garou is also immune to disease and poison, as well as any
corrupting effects from the touch of Wyrm entities. This Gift
is taught by a Bear-spirit.

System: The Garou spends one Gnosis point and rolls
Stamina + Survival (difficulty 7); the effects last one day per
success. She does not need to eat, drink or rest, and she gains
three extra points of Stamina, even if this takes the Attribute
over 10. She also ignores wound penalties. In addition, when-
evershe is about to die or be defeated, she automatically regains
one Willpower point.

swallow things or beings whole. A werewolf with this Gift is
even capable of gulping down an entire buffalo, ora whole pack
of Black Spiral Dancers, only to regurgitate the creatures lat

Children of Gaia

The Children of Gaia believe the Goddess has charged
them with bringing peace to the world and showing the Garou
that the path of hatred and war only serves the Wyrm. They
recognize the unity of all life as part of Gaia. Their Gifts heal
and calm or rouse nature to their aid.

‘w Mother's Touch (Level One) — As the Level One
Theurge Gift.

‘w Resist Pain (Level One) — As the Level One Philodox
Gift.

*w Calm (Level Two) — The werewolf can pull the hate
right out of a body. This Gift is taught by a Unicorn-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and rolls
Charisma + Empathy (difficulty of the target's Willpower).
Each success removes one of the target's Rage points, though
lost Rage can be regained normally.

‘w Luna's Armor (Level Two) — The werewolf
invokes the moon's sacred powers of protection to
repel hisenemy'sattacks. This Gift is taught by a Lune.

Chapter Six: Traits

6). Each success allows the Garou to add one to his Stamina for
the purpose of soaking damage (including damage from silver).
The effect lasts for one scene.

tw Dazzle (Level Three) — The werewolf can flood a
target's senses with visions of Gaia's true splendor. This Gift is
taught by a Unicorn-spirit.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Empathy (difficulty of
the target's Willpower). As long as the target is not attacked,
he stands mutely in awe for the remainder of the scene. This
Gift may only be attempted once per target per scene.

tw Halo of the Sun (Level Five) — The Child of Gaia
speaks an old word sacred to the Sun and is immediately
surrounded by a sphere of blazing sunlight. Those Wyrm-
creatures who fear the sun may flee before the brightly shining
Garou. This Gift is taught by a spirit allied to Helios, the Sun
Celestine.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point; the effect lasts
for one scene. The damage of any of the werewolf's hand-to-
hand attacks is increased by two dice; alternately, the Garou
can cause aggravated damage when in Homid or Glabro form.
Anyone directly facing the werewolf adds three to all attack
difficulties because of the glare, and any vampires within 20
yards suffer damage as if exposed to true sunlight.


SSS

The Fianna have strong ties to the spirit world as a result of
their ancient mystic heritage. The greatest allies of the Fianna
are the faeries of the Old World, but their aid and teachings are
lost to the Fianna of the Savage West.

‘w Persuasion (Level One) — As the Level One Homid Gift.

‘w Resist Toxin (Level One) — The Fianna have a long
practice of brewing whatever they can find to come up with
new forms of libations. Sometimes this has meant a fresh batch
of hooch that was outright dangerous — but the Fianna have
learned to adapt. A Garou with this Gift takes no damage from
poisons or diseases of any sort, including many Wyrm-toxins.
Some Toad-spirits and Plant-spirits may teach this Gift.

System: The Garou rolls Stamina + Survival (difficulty 6)
to resist the disease or poison; only one success is required.

w Glib Tongue (Level Two) — Some people only hear
what they want to hear, and this Gift lets a Fianna help them

along by making anything she says what the target wants tog!

hear. Whatever the werewolf says, even gibberish, will
heartily agreed with, although only the target of the Gift
know what she thinks the Garou is saying. (The Garou
have a clue what the target thinks she's saying.) Any one.
many Trickster-spirits may teach this Gift.

‘w Howl of the Banshee (Level Two) — The Fiann:
that the wail of the Banshee, a terrifying spirit of the dea
means that someone will die before the sun rises. A Garo)

System: The player spends a Gnosis point; the ai
throws back his head and howls. All who hear the howl, (re
or foe, must make Willpower rolls (difficulty 8; 6 for friends of
the Garou) or flee in terror for one turn per success.

W Prospector's Luck (Level Three) — In the Old World,
the Fianna learned how to find the secret places where faeries
hid riches in the earth. This Gift allows a Garou to sense
deposits of gold and silver within a general area (a couple of
mile radius). This Gift is taught by Rock-spirits.

System: The player spends a point of Willpower and rolls
Perception + Alertness (difficulty 7). Each success gives the
Garou more information. For example, a single success would
let the Fianna know that valuable minerals are in the area; two
successes would indicate the type, while three would give the
Fianna a general indication of where the precious metal was
located. With five successes, the Garou could walk over to a
stream bed and pluck outa gold-covered rock. This Gift cannot
locate gold or silver in an area where the metals aren't present.
Fianna can only attempt this Gift once in any given place.

‘w Balor's Gaze (Level Four) — When a Fianna invokes
this Gift, one of her eyes emits a baleful crimson glow, seeming
to burn with the fires of damnation. Anyone without the
resolve to meet Balor's Gaze without flinching suffers wracking
pains. A Pain-spirit teaches this Gift.

System: The player spends one Gnosis and one Rage
point, and rolls Perception + Occult (difficulty 8). For the

glances must equal or exceed the Garou's successes on a
Willpower roll (difficulty 8) or double over in agony. Such
a foe is treated as "Crippled" (-5 to actions) for the scene,
even though the foe takes no actual wounds. Already
Crippled enemies are effectively Incapacitated. If a foe
successfully resists, she is immune to the Gift for the dura-

grow up to three times her normal size or shrink to the size of
a small puppy. Chimerlings teach this Gift.

w Warp-Fury (Level Five) — The Fianna can channel all
his power into a desperate frenzy, the likes of which normal
humans have never seen — and lived. The werewolf draws
some of his own blood and tastes it to enter this state. Once he's
been driven into this frenzy, not even armies stand much of a
chance of stopping him.

System: The player spends three Rage points and rolls
Stamina + Primal-Urge, difficulty 4. The Fianna goes into
a warp-frenzy for one turn for each success. While in this
frenzied state, the Fianna takes no penalties from wounds,
gains +4 Strength, +3 Dexterity, +4 Stamina and three
extra Bruised Health Levels — all above and beyond any
normal Attribute modifiers for forms. In addition, he
regains at least one Rage point per turn. At the end of this
frenzy, the Fianna takes five Health Levels of aggravated
damage — that can't be soaked. If he survives, his
Rage and Willpower drop to 1 each until he's
rested for four hours. i)

Get of Fenris

The Get are violently protective of what they call their own, and consider themselves
the finest warriors of the Garou. Their Gifts either aid them in battle or allow them to take
good care of their beloved homesteads.

‘w Razor Claws (Level One) — As the Level One Ahroun Gift.

‘w Safe Haven (Level One) — The Garou employing this Gift knows if anything
suffering a Wyrm-taint come onto his property. This Gift is taught by an Owl-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis to establish this early warning system, and one
Gnosis per day to maintain it. To detecta Wyrmling entering the area, the character rolls
Wits + Perception (difficulty 7). Each success allows the character to more
clearly sense where the threat is coming from; one success might tell him that
athreat is coming from the east, whereas five successes could pinpoint the
exact location of the threat.

‘w Cry of the Killer (Level Two) — The werewolf lets out a fearsome
roar, terrifying opponents and cowing them into submission. Garou
hearing the roar are often cowed into submission; humans are flooded with
racial memories of the Impergium and are often overcome with instinctive.
terror. This Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

System: This Gift works automatically against humans and
animals. Any humans seeing the Garou after hear-
ing this cry react as if their Willpower were only
2 for purposes of the Delirium Chart. Against

4w Roar of Vengeance (Level Four) — The werewolf howls
at an opponent with impossible force, shattering bones and

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and one Will-
power point. He must also make a Rage roll, difficulty 6. Each
success causes two levels of aggravated damage to one opponent.
This Gift may only be used against minions of the Wyrm, and
any attempts to use the Gift improperly cause the character to

the character one permanent Glory renown.

‘w Fenris Bite (Level Five) — The Get's teeth become
incredibly sharp and her jaws impossibly strong, allowing her
to mangle limbs or even sever them completely. This Gift is
taught by an avatar of Fenris.

System: The playerspends one Rage point and rolls Strength
+ Medicine against a difficulty of the opponent's Stamina + 3.
The Garou's next bite, if it hits, will crush and mangle one of
the opponent's limbs, inflicting one additional level of damage
above what the character rolls. The target's limb is effectively
useless for the remainder of the scene, or longer if the target
can't regenerate. If five or more successes are scored on the
Strength + Medicine roll, the limb is completely severed.

‘w Honorable Death (Level Five) —The Garou chooses to
win against an opponent no matter the odds, and calls on
Fenris for aid. The Garou continues fighting long after he
should be dead, and doesn't fall until his enemy (or enemies)
dies with him. Obviously, this Gift is only ever used once. This
Gift is taught by an avatar of Fenris.

Werewolf: The Wild West

System: The Garou concentrates for one full turn, and then
expends all of his Gnosis in summoning Fenris' favor. Once
that's done, he suffers no penalties for his wounds, and won't fall
until his foe is dead. Despite the power of this Gift, the Get can't
seek an enemy he is notalready in direct combat with atthe time.
Once the battle is over, an avatar of Fenris comes down to devour
the body of the dead hero, taking all of his worldly possessions
with him. This Gift is only used when the character is already at
the brink of death, with no alternative for winning the battle.
Improper use of this Gift (i.e., attempting to settle a personal
vendetta instead of battling a minion of the Wyrm) immediately
kills the Garou who attempts to summon Fenris.

Iron Riders

The Iron Riders have a surprisingly accepting outlook on
the Weaver and her works, andas such have embraced modern
technology wholeheartedly. Their Gifts grant them power
over steam and iron, machinery and Weaver-spirits.

‘w Control Simple Machine (Level One) — The Iron Rider
can perceive and speak to the spirits in simplistic machines,
ordering them around to the Iron Rider's tastes. He can compel
doors to unbolt, hinges to swing open or shut, levers to flip and
so on. This Gift is taught by any technological spirit.

System: The player spends a Willpower point and rolls
Manipulation + Crafts (difficulty p- The werewolfs control

* Pulse of the Railroad (Level Two) — The Garou with
this Gift has learned the ways of the Weaver-spirits and can
commune with them. By placing her ear againsta railroad track,
she can hear anything happening anywhere along that rail as if
she were actually there. An Iron-spirit teaches this Gift.

System: By rolling Perception + Science, the

werewolf can attempt to listen to the Iron-spirits in


the rail. The difficulty depends on the distance away from the
target area. The difficulty starts at 4 with a range of 20 miles,
and increases by one for every 10 miles beyond 20 (40 miles
would be difficulty 6, etc.). The number of successes also
reflects the amount of details the Garou can discern from the
Iron-spirits' constant chatter.

w Repel Metal (Level Three) — The Iron Riders were the
first tribe to learn about the force of magnetism, and have
tapped its power to suit their own ends. With a mere gesture,
the werewolf can repel all ferrous metals in his immediate area,
sending them flying.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Strength
+ Science, difficulty 7. All objects made of ferrous metal within
30 feet of the Iron Rider immediately fly 20 feet farther away
from the Garou. This is no good against lead bullets or klaives,
but works very well against pistols and knives. The Garou can
repel objects weighing up to five pounds for every success on the
roll; three successes repel any iron or steel weighing 15 pounds
or less, for instance. If an object is in somebody's grasp (drawn
pistols, for instance), the object's owner can try to hold onto it
by making a resisted Strength roll against the Iron Rider.

‘w Reshape Object (Level Three) — As the Level
Homid Gift.

take the exact likeness of a particular human or
another werewolf. This is taught by a spirit of ilu:

System: The player spends one Gnosis

‘v Gift of the Iron Horse (Level Four) —
the rails of a railroad line, the Iron Rider cami
strength of the locomotive itself. ff

System: This Gift need not be activated; its effects are
automatic once learned. The Garou can run at double speed
without tiring, and gains an extra die to all her Physical
Attributes while touching railroad tracks. When on open
ground, her abilities return to normal.

‘w Calm the Flock (Level Five) — The Iron Rider can
actually walk among humans without accidentally evoking the
Curse or the Delirium. This Gift allows him to maintain a
"normal" life or evena family; however, the effects don't last all
that long. This Gift is taught by a homid Ancestor-spirit.

System: The player rolls Manipulation + Subterfuge (diffi-
culty 7). The number of successes indicates the number of
hours that the effect lasts.

W Quell the Storm (Level Five) — As the favored tribe of
the Weaver, Iron Riders are actually capable of imposing order
oneven the Storm Umbra. This Gift allows an Iron Rider elder
to calm and even dispel Umbral storms by countering their
Wyld energies with the power of the Weaver. Naturally,

several tribes, the Black Furies and Red Talons in

particular, resent werewolves who use this Gift to
advance the Weaver's influence.

System: The player spends two Gnosis and rolls Gnosis,
difficulty 8. The number of successes determines how effective
this Gift is; one success can slow a strong Umbral wind down
or banish an Umbral breeze, while five successes can convert a
full-blown Umbral storm into dead calm ina matter of seconds.
Ifused against Wyld-spirits, the roll is against a difficulty of the
spirit's Gnosis, and the spirit loses four Power for every success.
The werewolf cannot use this Gift more than once against the
same storm or spirit.

Red Talons

The all-lupus Red Talons enjoy powerful ties to the wilder-
ness, and have learned several Gifts to revenge themselves
against human depredations.

‘w Beast Speech (Level One) — As the Level One Galliard
Gift.

‘@ Beastmind (Level Two) — The Garou can reduce the
mental faculties of a target to those of an animal for a short
time. The victim doesn't necessarily become less intelligent,
but human thinking (logic, mathematics, language and the
like) becomes impossible. This Gift is taught by an avatar of
Griffin.

System: The Garou rolls Manipulation + Empathy against
a difficulty of the target's Willpower. The target is affected for
one turn per success.

‘@ Primal Howl (Level Two) — This Gift enables the Red
Talon to evoke deep-rooted fear in her enemies. The werewolf's
howl sounds as if it comes from several wolves (or Garou),
giving even Wyrm-creatures reason to pause.

System: The Garou rolls Stamina + Expression (difficulty
7). If the roll is successful, anyone who wishes to approach the
Garou must make a Willpower roll (difficulty 6) to overcome
instinctive fear. Each success after the first makes it sound as if
there is an additional howler and adds one to the difficulty of
the Willpower roll (maximum difficulty of 10).

‘@ Dust Squall (Level Three) — Developed by the
desert-dwelling Red Talons beyond the frontier, this new
Gift is otherwise not widely known. The werewolf can call
up a small twister of wind and sand to blind or scare
opponents. The wind is not large enough to knock full-sized
targets over, but will cause them some distress. This Gift is
taught by a spirit of the great desert.

Chapter Six: Traits 187


OXX

System: The Garou spends a Gnosis point and rolls Charisma
+ Primal-Urge, difficulty 6. Each success means the squall "at-
tacks" the Talon's opponents for one turn. Opponents besieged by
the squall add two to the difficulty of all dice rolls, and communi-
cation and vision become almost impossible over any distance.
‘w Trackless Waste (Level Three) — This Gift allows a
Garou who is at home ina certain area of wilderness (has spent
some time hunting and sleeping there) to disorient others who
enter the region. Humans and other travelers are completely
confused by this Gift, losing their bearings and becoming lost.
Compasses and maps only serve to confuse the issue fur-
ther, and even werewolves aren't immune. This Gift is
taught by any wilderness spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls
Intelligence + Primal-Urge (difficulty 6; 7 against lupus). He
may affect up to a two-mile radius for every success. Other
werewolves can avoid the effects by rolling Intelligence +

Primal-Urge themselves and obtaining equal or more suc-

cesses than the Red Talon. Humans and creatures of the
Wyrm get no such chance. The effect lasts for four hours.

W Quicksand (Level Four) — The Red Talon turns the
surrounding area into an unstable and viscous substance which
entrapsand hinders her foes. The hapless victims can suffocate

if not rescued. The Gift is taught by earth elementals.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls
Manipulation + Primal-Urge against a difficulty of 7. Each
success converts a 10-foot area of ground into dangerous
muck. Anyone in the area but the Garou has their move-
ment halved; weak individuals begin tosink. Trapped targets
can't attempt any activities requiring speed or balance, and
they may eventually suffocate.

w Sandstorm (Level Four) — A torrent of stinging and
blinding sand encompasses the enemies of the Garou. This
Gift is best performed in the desert, but can be attempted in
any area with loose dirt, twigs, sleet or the like. This Gift is
taught by a Desert-spirit or elemental.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Ma-
nipulation + Survival (difficulty 7 in the desert; 9 anywhere
else). The number of successes determines the number of
minutes in which the targets can do nothing but cover them-
selves and shelter as best they can from the stinging sand. A
botch indicates that the Talon himself is caught in the blast.
‘w Gaia's Vengeance (Level Five) — The Garou calls upon
her intimate connection to Gaia, inspiring the land itself to
enact righteous vengeance on her foes. Trees, rocks and other
natural features hinder, hamper and batter the Garou's en-
emies while the Red Talon watches. It's said that an avatar of
Gaia Herself is the only spirit who teaches this Gift.

System: The player spends one Gnosis and one Rage, and rolls
Charisma + Enigmas against a difficulty of the area's Gauntlet.
The Red Talon pleads with Gaia to cleanse the land, and

Gaia responds with whatever weapons the area has to

offer. The game effects are left to the Storyteller.

Garou, he becomes trapped in his Lupus form for the duration of
the current scene. If the target is a human or animal, it becomes
anormal wolf forever. The Gift is taught by a Wolf-spirit.

Shadow Lords

The Shadow Lords are dark, brooding sorts who feel an
almost unconscious need for dominance and submission. Their
Gifts allow them to intimidate others, put them in their place,
or help weed out the weak and unfit.

‘w Aura of Confidence (Level One) — The werewolf seems
surrounded by a dark halo of strength and command. Others find it
impossible to read her aura or detect any of her weaknesses; she
revealsno flaws whatsoever. This Gift is taught by an Ancestor-spirit.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Subterfuge (diffic
This must be rolled for each new encounter Otsituation i

‘@ Clap of Thunder (Level Two) — The Shadow Lord may
clap her hands together, generating a shockwave that can stun
unwary foes. This Gift is taught by a Stormcrow.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point. All those
within 10 feet of the Shadow Lord, friend and foe alike, must
score at least one success on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8) or
be stunned and unable to act for one turn; ona botch, they are
stunned for the duration of the scene. The Garou must be in
Homid, Glabro or Crinos form to employ this power.

‘@ Luna's Armor (Level Two) — As the Level Two
Children of Gaia Gift.

w Icy Chill of Despair (Level Three) — The Shadow Lord
can appear to grow larger, becoming a shadowy, terrible ver-
sion of himself. This frightening change in aspect cows and
intimidates any onlookers. This Gift is taught by a Stormcrow.

System: The Garou concentrates for a turn; the player spends a
Gnosis point and rolls Manipulation + Intimidation (difficulty 7). If
successful, anyone who means the Shadow Lord harm and actually
sees this terrifying vision must score at least as many successes as the
Garou on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8). Failure means that the
victims must spend Willpower to attack, take action against or even

verbally oppose the Garou. The Shadow Lord holdsno power
over his victims; they're merely too spooked to offer him
any resistance. This effect lasts for the rest of the scene.

%@ Shadow Cutting (Level Three) — The Shadow Lord
can wounda foe by striking his very shadow. This form of attack
is much more difficult to dodge, and can be a most convenient
attack form in some conditions — such as sundown. This Gift
is taught by a Night-spirit.

System: The werewolf must first spit into her opponent's
shadow and spend a Gnosis point. For the remainder of the
scene, the Shadow Lord may strike at her foe's shadow to wound
him. She may only use fetish weapons, silver or natural weaponry
such as claws to attack in this fashion; more mundane attacks
don't carry any impact. The victim has two fewer dice to dodge
attacks targeted at his shadow, and can't parry them at all. The
Storyteller may even give the shadow's attackers extra dice in
situations where the shadow prevents an even more convenient
target; for example, if the victim is standing in an alleyway out
of reach, but his shadow falls at the Garou's feet.

‘w Open Wounds (Level Four) — The Shadow Lord may
curse the next wound he inflicts on a target, making it bleed
ly. This Gift is taught by a Pain-spirit.

‘w Shadow Pack (Level Five) — The werewolf may sum-
mona shadowy duplicate of himself, or even multiple shadows,
to stand beside him in battle. His shadow-duplicates possess
the same powers as he does, and can fight just as fiercely. This
Gift is taught by a spirit of Night or a Chimerling.

System: The player must spend a variable number of Gnosis
pointsand succeed in a Gnosis roll (difficulty 8). For each Gnosis
point spent, the Shadow Lord summons an exact duplicate of
himselfto fight by hisside. These Garou have identical Attributes
and Abilities, but not fetishes; they may not use Gnosis or
Willpower, and each has only one Health Level (any attack that
is not soaked destroys it). The shadow-Garou last for one scene.

‘@ Wounding Lies (Level Five) — Although Shadow
Lords have no qualms about bending the truth to serve their
own ends, they dislike being lied to, especially by lessers. This
Gift allows the werewolf to force truth from a subject; if the
target lies to the Garou, wounds appear across his body. He who
prevaricates, dies. This Gift is taught by a Pain-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Cha-
risma + Intimidation, difficulty of the subject's Willpower. If
he succeeds, the link is established. From then on, every fib


the subject tells inflicts a Health Level of aggravated damage
on the liar; this damage is not soakable. Even half-truths open
small, stinging but not life-threatening wounds. Garou may
overcome the effect by spending as much Gnosis as the
interrogator rolled successes. Unless the victim succeeds ata
Willpower roll, difficulty 8, he will probably start blurting out
the truth after the first wound or two — particularly if the
Shadow Lord tells him that keeping silent will kill him, too
(alie, but in most Shadow Lords' eyes, a justifiable one). This
Gifts lasts for a scene.

Silent Striders

The Silent Striders are no strangers to the Umbra, and their
skills of travel and survival are nigh-legendary. Their Gifts
make them fleet of foot and allow them access to places
untouchable by other Garou.

@ Sense Wyrm (Level One) — As the Level One Metis
Gift.

"@ Speed of Thought (Level One) — The Garou may double
her land speed. This Gift is taught by a Horse- or Roadrunner-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point; and the effect
lasts one scene.

W Hide in Plain Sight (Level Two) — As the Level Two
Ragabash Gift.

‘W Messenger's Fortitude (Le

System: The playerspendsa point of Gnosis and the werewolf
may do nothing except run for the duration of the Gift. By

Messenger's Fortitude on another being. The Gift lasts as many
days as the (breed form) Stamina of the character affected.

‘w Gaia's Resilience (Level Three) — The Garou adapts to
accommodate any surroundings. While under the effects of
this Gift, she is unaffected by pressure, temperature, atmo-
spheric conditions and other hostile environmental factors.
Poison and disease likewise have no effect on her, though she
may be harmed by concentrated flame or radiation. This Gift
is taught by Bear- and Armadillo-spirits.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Stamina
+ Survival (difficulty 7). The effects last for one hour per
success and may be extended by spending another Gnosis and
rolling again. The Garou may also bestow this Gift upon
another; each affected companion requires a Gnosis point
expenditure, as well.

‘w Leap the Chasm (Level Three) — The Strider can jump
incredible distances in one bound. This Gift is taught by
Jackrabbit-spirits.

System: The player spends a Willpower point and rolls
Strength + Athletics (difficulty 6). Each success enables the
werewolf to leap 100 feet.

4 Attunement (Level Four) — By standing in a particular
area of the wilderness, the character can commune with the
spirits of that area, thus getting an overview of what exists or has
happened in the area—rough population, secret trails, places of
note and so on. This Gift is taught by an Owl- or Fox-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Percep-
tion + Survival (difficulty 6). The amount of information
garnered depends on the number of successes. On a botch, the
spirits lie.

sw Windrunner (Level Four) — The werewolf may run at 10
times normal speed. The Garou must do nothing but run for the
duration of this Gift: eight full hours. After the effects of this Gift
wear off, the Strider is famished and must eat immediately or risk
frenzy (and worse... .). This Gift is taught by an Air-spirit or Lune.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point and rolls Stamina
+ Athletics (difficulty 7). Failure means the Gift simply does
not work, while a botch only bestows the hunger of the
aftermath on the poor Garou....

‘@ Gate of the Moon (Level Five) — This power only works
at night when the moon is at least partially visible. The
werewolf opens a special, temporary Moon Bridge, which
allows instantaneous travel to her destination. This Gift is only
taught by Lunes.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point for every 100
. e Garou to travel, and rolls Intelligence +

ey,to an unknown place would
demand a diffic . Success enables the werewolf to
travel to that place instantly. Upon arrival, the Garou will be
disoriented for a turn unless the player scores at least three
successes. A botch means that the character is hurled through
the Umbra in a random direction, arriving far from his
destination (or somewhere inconvenient, like at the bottom
of a river). The Garou may take others with her at a Gnosis
cost of one per person.

*w Reach the Umbra (Level Five) — The Garou may pierce
the Gauntlet and instantly transport herself into or out of the
Umbraat will; she has no need fora reflective surface. This Gift
is taught by an Owl-spirit.

System: The Garou simply wills herself to step sideways.
She does not have to make a Gnosis roll and is never "caught"
while using this Gift.

Silver Fangs

The Silver Fangs are the kings of the Garou, and have the
majestic power to back up their claims. They maintain their
Gifts are extensions of Gaia's will and nothing less.

‘v Eminent Domain (Level One) — The werewolf using
this Gift gives others pause by simply entering a room. Crowded
rooms will fall silent and the weak-hearted will fall back
from the Garou's very presence.This Gift is taught by
a Falcon-spirit.


System: The player rolls Appearance + Intimidation (dif-
ficulty 7). The number of successes indicates the level of
attention the Garou attracts. One or two successes indicates
the werewolf has been noticed; three or more successes indi-
cates the targets are seriously impressed by her appearance. If
the player scores more successes than a witness's Willpower,
that person must retreat from the werewolf's majestic presence.

‘w Sense Wyrm (Level One) — As the Level One Metis
Gift.

‘w Awe (Level Two) — The Fang proves her right to rule
and mastery over a target by assuming a regal bearing. This Gift
is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The player rolls Appearance + Empathy (difficulty
of the target's Willpower). If this roll is successful, the difficul-
ties of all Social rolls made by the Garou against that target are
reduced by one, and the target's difficulty to hit the Garou in
combat is increased by one. This Gift is ineffective against
Garou of higher Rank.

Children of Gaia Gift.

that he is Gaia's Chosen Warrior and the instrument of Her

‘W Gaia's Favor (Level Four) — The Garou may invoke

System: Before rolling for the action in question, the player
may spend a point of Gnosis. If any botches turn up on the dice
roll, they are ignored; do not count them toward the successes
or failures of the roll.

‘@ Mastery (Level Four) — The werewolf may command
other Garou, even Black Spiral Dancers, to do his bidding. This
Gift is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The player rolls Charisma + Leadership (diffi-
culty of the target's Wits + 3). If the roll is successful, the
Garou may then issue a command to the target, who must
carry out the command (unless it is blatantly suicidal). This
Gift affects only Garou.

w Luna's Might (Level Five) — The Silver Fang trans-

invincible warrior. This Gift is taught by Lunes and

+ Ancestor-spirits.

System: The Garou concentrates for an entire turn and her
player spends a Gnosis point. Using this Gift confers immunity
to silver upon the user. In addition, the Gift user is assumed to
have the equivalent of the Level Three Ahroun Gift, Silver
Claws. She also gains two extra Stamina levels and one extra
Health Level (if she suffers only one Health Level worth of
damage in combat, she is unharmed after Luna's Might ex-
pires). This Gift lasts one scene.

‘w Whelp the Insolent (Level Five) — The Fang prevents
her victim from using any special or supernatural abilities. This
Gift is commonly used to teach young Garou their place in
addition to besting foes. It is taught by a Falcon-spirit.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and rolls
Gnosis (difficulty of the victim's Stamina + 3). Each success
removes all special abilities (aggravated damage, claws, Gifts,
vampire Disciplines and Blood Pool, mage Spheres, regenera-
tive powers, special attacks, etc.) from the victim for one turn.

Stargazers

The Stargazers bring a rare form of wisdom to the Savage
West, where greed, anger and selfishness rather than under-
standing and truth inspire actions. The Stargazers' Gifts help
them show Garou and humans insights into the world, but also
deli er its message with a righteous claw.
One) — The Garou is able to walk along
atious path without slipping or falling. This
untain passes, ropes and telegraph lines,
slippery or wet. This Gift is taught by

n when they,
ange Wind: known to the Stargazers.

"System: N@ rolls are made nor points spent to traverse a
narrow path. Difficulties of climbing are reduced by three. The
effect lasts for one scene.

‘w Threads of the Weaver (Level One) — With this Gift
a Stargazer can sense the threads woven through reality by the
Weaver. The werewolf thus knows if the effects of the Weaver
have been felt in an area, but also senses the direction in which
those spirits head, and perhaps for what purpose. This Gift is
taught by a Spider-spirit.

System: The player rolls Perception + Enigmas (difficulty
6) and the character concentrates on his surroundings. The
Garou gets a glimpse into the Penumbra, but to see only the
webs strung by the Weaver and its minions. The character
then knows if the Weaver has affected an area recently, if it
still does and sometimes if it will in the near future. On a roll
of Wits + Enigmas (difficulty 8) the character gets a sense of
how the Weaver affects the area, and an inkling of the
repercussions of that effect.

Stargazers have used this Gift to sense where railroads,
telegraph wires and other trappings of civilization will pen-
etrate the West. The power can also be used to know where the
Storm Eater has touched the Penumbra and the material world,
and the Gift might be used to follow the trail of the Storm Eater
or those of its creations.

‘w Counting Coup (Level Four) — By touching an opponent
in combat, a Stargazer can break the combatant's will and drive
him from the fray or force him to surrender. This Gift was learned
from the Pure Ones, who no longer have time or patience forsuch
gentle tactics, and is taught by a Pure One Ancestor-spirit.

Uktena

Delvers into the secrets of the Wyrm, binders of its minions,
the subtle Uktena have many animal powers and powerful
spirit medicine.

W Cloud of Night (Level One) — The Garou calls into
being a cloud of impenetrable darkness. The Gift is taught by
a spirit of Night.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and rolls
Gnosis against a variable difficulty (3 for twilight, 6 for indoors,
9 for high noon). For each success, a 10-foot by 10-foot area of
the Garou's choice falls under the effect of the dark cloud.

‘w Sense Medicine (Level One) — The werewolf can
sense any sort of native medicine as well as other forms of
"magic," whether Garou Gifts and rituals, fetishes, spirit
Charms, vampiric Thaumaturgy, Spheres of magick used by
human mages, faerie Glamour or other magical activity.
Those so gifted may sense the presence and general strength;
only basic information — such as whether it is faerie
Glamour or mortal magick — is conveyed, not specific
Spheres or individual powers. This Gift is taught by a
servant of Uktena.

System: The player rolls Perception + Enigmas with the
difficulty based on the strength and subtlety of the magic. The
radius is 10 feet per success rolled.

‘w Flight of the Raven (Level Two) — The werewolf gains
the power to hover and float in the air. This Gift is taught by
any Bird-spirit.

System: The player spends a Gnosis point; the werewolf
may then fly at 20 mph for one hour. Maneuvering requires a
Dexterity + Occult roll; difficulties of all complex actions such
as combat are increased by two.

‘w Sense the Tunneler's Passage (Level Two) — The
Garou can sense nearby manifestations of the Wyrm,
though she may not tell exactly what sort of creature is
giving off the reek. Once she has picked up the Wyrm-
scent, she may attempt to track it if it has moved beyond
the initial area searched. This Gift is taught by any spirit
servant of Mother Earth.

System: This Gift works much like the Gift: Sense Wyrm,
save that the Uktena can track Wyrm-creatures. To track a
Wyrm-creature once the trail is found, the player must spend
a Gnosis point and roll Perception + Primal-Urge (difficulty
8). Even one success allows her to track the creature for as far
as the creature can travel in 10 minutes. More rolls may be

made to pick up the trail after that, but once the roll

is failed, the trail is lost. It is also lost if the creature

W Manifest the Vision (Level Five) — The werewolf with

This Gift is taught by a Chimerling.

Werewolf: The Wild West

System: The player makes an extended roll of Intelligence
+ Performance (difficulty 8) to create any form of life she can
imagine. Each success provides one point of Traits to assign
to the creature. The Uktena can take as long as she desires to
form the being, thus accumulating successes and strengthen-
ing it from turn to turn; but once she stops, the being of her
vision takes form and she must spend Gnosis to keep it
manifested. Manifestation costs one point per scene if the
Dream Being remains relatively inactive, such as keeping
guard or grinding corn, or one point per turn if the being
engages in combat. Botched rolls evoke uncontrolled, often
hostile Nightmare Beings who, though they cost no Gnosis,
remain as long as they desire.

Wendigo

In the Savage West, the Wendigo dig in their heels to
resist the white invaders. They draw power from their totem
and the frozen north, but also possess Gifts that link them
é land — a land under attack. They call on that
ce to drive away the Wyrmcomers and restore the
d Earth.

‘e Bitter Wind (Level Two) — The Wendigo may call up

"a painfully cold blast of wind to strike his foes. This Gift is

taught by a spirit servant of Wendigo.

System: The player spends a Willpower point; to direct
the wind in combat, he rolls Dexterity + Occult (difficulty 6).
Anyone hit by the wind loses two dice from all actions taken
that turn, and has one fewer die on the next turn. The wind
mightalso knock foes into canyons, down mine shafts and the
like. The range is calculated according to the modifiers for
firearms. The medium range is 20 yards; twice that range (40
yards) is the farthest the wind can be directed, and the
difficulty for such a distance is increased by one. On the other
hand, attacks made at targets within a yard of the Garou are
considered "point blank" (difficulty 4). The wind lasts for the
entire scene.

4@ Dust Storm (Level Two) — As she sings to the Wind-
spirits, the Wendigo summons a driving cloud of wind and dust.
Although no one (including the werewolf or her friends) is
immune to the dust, any Wendigo knows that a brown haze on
the horizon means trouble.

System: The player calls Gnosis, to call up the wind
and dirt. The roll's difficulty depends on the area. *

Once called, the storm whips through the area for two turns
per success, blinding everyone (half Perception Dice Pools),
fouling machinery and coating everything in a fine, gritty dust.
In colder regions, snow or ice substitutes for dust, while leaves
and bark make up a woodland storm.

‘w Sky Running (Level Three) — The Wendigo gains the
ability to run at 50 mph through the skies, but must keep running
if he doesn't want to fall. The werewolf leaves a track of fire in the
sky as he runs. This Gift is taught by a spirit servant of Wendigo.

System: The Garou concentrates for one turn; the player
spends a Willpower point. The Gift lasts for four hours and may
be replenished by further Willpower expenditure.

Call the Cannibal Spirit (Level Four) — The werewolf
must chant and dance for a full hour under the night sky to
invoke this Gift. If the summons is successful, an avatar of
Wendigo answers. The avatar tracks down a victim of the
Garou's choosing (the Garou must have an article or part of the
victim) to eat his heart.

System: The player spends one Gnosis point and one Rage
point and rolls Charisma + Occult (difficulty 8). If the roll
botches, or the Wendigo avatar is thwarted in its mission, it
will return to kill the summoner. Statistics for the Wendigo-
spirit are given in the Appendix (pg. 275).

w Spirits of Decay (Level Four) — The inventions of the
invaders are an affront to the Old Ways. This Gift breaks
them down to the elements they came from. Many spirits,
from the Termite to the West Wind, teach this blessing to
their favored ones.

System: The player rolls Manipulation + Enigmas and

spends a Rage point; the Wendigo can then make one

unliving item crumble into dust or debris. The roll's

difficulty depends on the strength of the item.

Items made of several materials (guns, houses) may go
by the weakest material involved; a steel axe is useless
without its wooden handle. Really large targets (trains,
boulders) may require five or 10 successes to destroy. The
item falls apart one turn after the Gift is used. This Gift
does not make a vampire decay completely, but he suffers
one aggravated Health Level for each success against him
(difficulty 8).

w The Freezing Night (Level Five) — Wendigo is the
totem of the frozen North. At night and in winter, he steals
across the plains, freezing the land and anyone on it. This
Gift calls Wendigo's power to the Garou; through it, he may
call a killing ice storm down. It is taught by an avatar of
Wendigo himself.

System: The player spends two Gnosis points and rolls
illpower (difficulty.6)eBach'Success inflicts two normal

bh LewélS"against everyone in the path of the storm.
his may be soaked, but even the hardiest characters will be
incomfortable. Characters with thick winter clothing or
magical protection (like the Song of the Seasons Gift)
reduce that damage by half. The storm itself comes in a few
turns after the Gift is used (time depends on climate); it
begins as a cold wind and rises to a frigid gale before
subsiding at the end of the scene (or one hour). The nexus
of The Freezing Night covers roughly half a mile, and kills
small animals, plants or unprotected humans at the
Storyteller's option. As the name suggests, The Freezing
Night can only be called at night.

v Tornado Rider (Level Five) — Carried on the wings
of Thunderbird, a Wendigo may call up a twister, then ride
it, safe from harm, as it tears up the works of man. This Gift
comes from Thunderbird himself, who often cleanses the
iron-stink from his lands by force.

System: The player spends two Gnosis points and rolls
Willpower (difficulty 6). The twister starts to take shape
one turn later and hits the ground two turns after that. Once
the tornado lands, the Wendigo can leap aboard and ride
the winds without injury. The twister lasts three + (number
of successes) turns and effectively demolishes anything the
Storyteller rules would be destroyed. The player may suggest
a path, but cannot influence the tornado once it comes.
Anyone caught by the twister takes five Health Levels (or
more) in normal damage before being flung off in a random
direction (which'll probably cause even more hurt). Fortu-
nately, this can be soaked.

Rites

Itis only through the strength of our spirit that we have been able
to survive in this new land. Our rites, handed down from the first
teachings of Gaia, are strong and deep. The spirits here remember
Her voice, though they have long been sundered from us.

— Atticus Cleanclaw, Silver Fang Philodox

Unlike Gifts, which are the personal manifestations of a
Garou's power, purity and connection to Gaia, rites
emonies in which many werewolves celebrate their oneness
with the earth together. The supernatural power of rites is less
obvious than that of Gifts, and often less immediate. Rites are
not less powerful than Gifts, merely deeper, more subtle and
often more enduring.

re cer-

Rites connect the Garou to Gaia, to each other, to the spirits
and to their ancestors in an unchanging sequence which
stretches back beyond remembering. Werewolves, although
they are embodiments of change, are conservative creatures.
Rites are their heritage and more. Usually performed by a
Theurge or Galliard (depending on the type and purpose of the
rite), although any Garou can lead a rite, these ceremonies are
the focus of a Garou's spiritual existence, the renewal of their
essence. Each phase of a Garou's life is marked by a rite, from
the initial Rite of Passage to the posthumous Wake Rite.

Ritesrepresentcommonalties and differences between Garou
at the same time. Whilst all tribes know and practice a version
of the Rite of Passage, the details of these ceremonies vary
wildly between the tribes. The harsh and dangerous journey
required of a new Get of Fenris cub could not be more different
than the drinking competition among the Fianna, or the
cryptic visionquesting of the Uktena. Yet each of these rites has
the same purpose; all descend from the first Rite of Passage,
held in the presence of Gaia, or so the Garou believe.

Types of Rites

Although all rites are in a sense the same ceremony, the
differences in detail between them can be expressed in
terms of various groups of rites. It should be noted that
werewolves don't make these distinctions, seeing things in
a more organic whole. Some rites can be enacted by one
Garou; others require many. Some rites must be enacted in
the Umbra, others at the caern, still more can be per-
formed anywhere. The broad categories into which rites
are grouped are: Accord, Caern, Death, Frontier, Mystic,
Punishment, Pure Ones, Renown, Seasonal and Minor.
Each type of rite has basic requirements and specific
requirements which the ritemaster must fulfill in order for
the ceremony to be successful.

Any Garou can learn any rite, although some kinds of rites
are more commonly known by certain auspices. If the character
is a Theurge, he is expected to know many rites,
especially those directly concerned with the manipu-

lation of and communication with spirits. ®

Werewolf: The Wild West
D


= =
Enacting a Rite

The ritual process of the rite is a complicated and drawn-out
affair. In the following descriptions of rites, only the bare bones,
requirements and effects are laid out. The Storyteller should fill
in color and detail, describing the patterns of the particular
dance, the herbs on the fire, the words of the spiritsongand other
aspects which add to the flavor and image of the experience.

Generally a group of Garou participates, led by a single
werewolf, the ritemaster. The ritemaster's responsibility is to
ensure that the rite succeeds; she generally reaps the greatest
rewards or suffers the greatest harm, depending on the
ceremony's outcome. It is her Attributes and Abilities that are
used to make the roll which determines the rite's outcome. In
general, three Garou are required to enact a rite. A ritemaster
canattempta rite with fewer Garou or even on his own, but this
increases the difficulty by one (except for certain rites which
need only one Garou — see the descriptions of the individual
rites). For every five Garou above the number required, the
difficulty of the rite is reduced by one, to a minimum of 3.

Rites generally take about 10 minutes per level to complete,
though this duration can vary. A particularly skilled ritemaster
who has a reason to hurry might be able to complete a rite in
as short as half that time. Garou do not generally hurry rites,
however, as they are part of a natural cycle, and not supernatu-
ral powers to be used in a crisis as are Gifts.

= =
Learning a Rite

Ritesare ancestral knowledge and property among werewolves.
They usually can only be learned from an elder who knows and
is willing to teach them. Werewolf elders don't jealously guard
the secrets of rites, but neither do they reveal them haphazardly
toany cub whoasks. Cubs have to win the respect of an elder and
convince him that his hard-earned knowledge would be safe and
in responsible hands. In some cases, the elder might ask some
kind of material payment for his services, perhaps in the form of
talens, but this isn't all that common. It's generally enough that
acubdemonstrates that he'sa worthy recipient of the knowledge
(determined by past deeds and appropriate roleplaying); if the
elder calls for payment, it's more likely to be in the form of some
service or action. If the elder judges a cub unworthy of the
knowledge — and his judgment is stricter for more important
rites — then he determines a task that must be accomplished,
not one which directly benefits him, but one which benefits
Gaia and proves the cub's bravery.

Once the elder has consented to teach the rite, the student
must succeed in learning it. The Garou must possess a Rituals
knowledge at least equal to the level of the rite, representing
the necessary background and attunement to begin the pro-
cess. This process isa lengthy one of study, trial and error during
which the Garou can do little else but meditate, live alone in

the wild and practice the new knowledge, returning
regularly for further instruction. It takes at least one
& week per level of such activity to learn a rite (three

days total for minor rites). This is represented as an extended
action in which the player rolls Intelligence + Rituals against
a difficulty of 7. The number of successes required equals the
level of the rite. Only one roll is made for each learning period
(one roll every three weeks for a Level Three rite). If the player
gets no successes, the Garou must spend a Willpower point to
continue the study — otherwise she loses all knowledge, and
the teacher will not share any more rites with the failed student
unless she atones for her incompetence in some way. If the roll
botches, that cub is unlikely to ever learn that rite, and that
teacher certainly will not teach her again. Otherwise, the
character finishes her period of study with her first enactment
of the rite, in which her entire sept participates.

A character can begin the game with some learned rites by
purchasing the Rites Background. If so, he's assumed to have
undergone the above process. It's suggested that these rites be of
low level; it doesn't make sense for a Garou to have mastered a
Level Five rite without having knowledge of any other rites. After
the beginning of play, rites can only be gained in the way described
above, and never by the expenditure of experience points.

Unlearned Rites: A character in dire straits can attempt to
enact a rite in which he's taken part, but which he doesn't
know. This is extremely difficult and dangerous, not to men-
tion disrespectful to elder Garou and to the spirits involved. A
Garou who has no knowledge of any rites can't try this at all.
The difficulty is three higher than normal and costs double the
amount of Gnosis. Twice as many Garou are required to gain
bonuses or avoid penalties (as described above) and the results
of a botch are twice as bad.

Auspice Roles

Although any werewolf can learn any rite and the spirits
make no distinctions between auspices, Garou themselves
believe it is right and proper for certain auspices to be masters
of certain rites. Sometimes it is the eldest Garou ina sept, or the
Garou whois the center of the rite who leads the ceremony, but
more often the decision is made on the basis of auspice first and
rank second when it comes to choosing the ritemaster.

Theurges, Philodox and Galliards are most likely to be called
upon tolead rites, and are trained for the roleand the honor. Not
all of these Garou are specialized in conducting rites, butall have
at least a working knowledge, and any character from one of
these auspices is expected to have some knowledge of the process
and to know a few basic rites (one or two points of Rituals
Knowledge). In general, Theurges tend to learn those Mystic
and Caern Rites which deal fully with spirits and the Umbra,
Philodox tend to learn rites with a political bent, such as Rites
of Accord and Punishment, and Galliards usually learn rites of
celebration or mourning, such as the Wake Rite, which give
them an opportunity to express their artistic souls.

Any Garou can learn rites, and Ragabash and Ahroun can
and do lead the sept in certain rites in time of need and danger.
These auspices gain little Renown asa general rule for doing so,
but the Ragabash who steps into the role of the Theurge when
