Warpers

Warpers, or Chimerlings as the Stargazers call them, are
Jagglings of the totem Chimera. These oddities change shape
as quickly and frequently as a wolf breathes, never holding any
pattern for more than a few seconds. The Uktena believe the
Warpers are responsible for many of the prophetic dreams the
Garou experience. Sadly, what these dreams are attempting to
convey is seldom clear to any of the Garou. The Chimerlings
often teach Gifts to the Garou, and are considered among the
wisest of spirits.
Willpower 3, Rage 5, Gnosis 10, Power 35
Charms: Airt Sense, Reform, Shapeshift

Englings
Englings are Jagglings of Gaia and carry a part of Her within
them. They are spirits of Gnosis, and Garou often ritualistically
hunt themdown during moots. OnceaEnglinghas been captured,
the Garou ritualistically thanks the spirit for giving of itselfso that
the Garou may continue to defend Gaia. If this ritual is completed
properly, all Garou involved regain
all their Gnosis. The Engling
then disappears, Reform-

concepts, suchas war, death, pain,

The Wendigo are convinced that these Manitou are little more
than unformed spirits of vengeance, waiting for the chance to
grow in powerand help destroy the Wyrm-Bringers. Whatever the
Epiphlings truly are, they are normally encountered as Gafflings,
and are frequently bound into talens and fetishes. The Storyteller
is encouraged to create statistics for the Manitou, for each is
different from the next.

Wyldlings

Wyldlings are spirits of the Wyld, the uncontrollable forces
that created the universe given life and mobility. Most of the
Wyldlings stay far away from the Gaia-Realm, preferring to
reside where the Weaver and the Wyrm have not yet gained
any influence. From time to time these powerful spirits do
break through the Pattern Web and enter the physical world,
where they cause chaos and shatter the Weaver's patterns
wherever possible. Regrettably, they now have a great enemy
in the Wyld-devouring Storm Eater, who feeds on these
completely unpredictable spirits whenever possible. All
Wyldlings have the Charm: Break Reality.

Cyclones

Cyclones are some of the most powerful Wyldlings, and are as
unpredictableas the storms from which they get theirname. These
massive swirling pools of chaotic energy are feared and respected
by Garou and spirits alike, for theirs is the ability to completely
alter reality. Cyclones used to rampage freely across the physical
world — and still do, occasionally, in the company of Wyld
mages — but now spend most of their time in the Deep Umbra.

AsaCyclone iscomprised ofall thingsreal and unreal —fire
and water, wood, glass and everything else at once — any
damage it inflicts is considered aggravated.

Willpower 8, Rage 10, Gnosis 10, Power 80

Charms: Airt Sense, Break Reality, Cleanse the Blight,

Disorient, Materialize, Reform, Shapeshift, Umbraquake

Lesser Wyldlings

While not as powerful as the Cyclones, lesser Wyldlings are
still very dangerous. Most appear as shifting, hazy turbulent
swirls of color. While they occasionally act as spirit guides, all
werewolves agree that the Wyldlings aren't to be trusted.
Willpower 4, Rage 7, Gnosis 6, Power 40
Charms: Airt Sense, Break Reality, Materialize, Reform,
Shapeshift

Weaver-spirits

The spirit servants of the Weaver are responsible for what
we call reality. They strive to maintain order in an endless
struggle against the Wyld. Most Weaver-spirits resemble spi-
ders, and normally inhabit the Pattern Web. Some are rumored

to look like odd geometric patterns. But few, save the
Tron Riders, claim to have seen them. All Weaver-

Y spirits have the Charm: Solidify Reality.

Pattern Spiders
Pattern Spiders are the most common of the Weaver's

servants. These large, crystalline creatures are primarily work-

ers, constantly adding to and reinforcing the Pattern Web.

They are docile for the most part, but instantly attack anything

that violates the Pattern Web's integrity, calcifying the offend-

ing creature and adding it to the Pattern Web.

Willpower 6, Rage 4, Gnosis 6, Power 25

Charms: Calcify, Solidify Reality

Chaos Monitors

These powerful spirits seem to serve the Weaver as shock troops.
Bearinga powerful resemblance to gigantic glass scorpions, the Chaos
Monitors often visit the Gaia-Realm, working to destroy anything
that doesn't fit within the constant, steady reality the Weaver's
worked so hard to create. The Iron Riders believe the Chaos
Monitors are the Weaver's answer toa world that is often too Wyld.

When materialized in the physical world, these spirits defend
the Weaver's creations, destroying anything that attempts to
harm what the Weaver builds. Their entry into the Gaia-Realm
is not gentle, however, and has led to several weak spots in the
Gauntlet. Some werewolves guess that this is how caerns first
came about, but most scoff at the notion. Of late, the Storm
Eater's influence has begun to affect these brutal spirits. Some are
coming out of the Umbra with glazed, blackened carapaces and
with a very noticeable Wyrm-taint. The Iron Riders are justifi-
ably concerned about this new development.
Willpower 7, Rage 10, Gnosis 9, Power 60
Charms: Airt Sense, Calcify, Healing, Materialize, Reform,
Solidify Reality, Spirit Static

Wire Hoppers

Wire Hoppers are a recent discovery among the Weaver-
spirits. These odd, multilegged Gafflings are only found running
across the wires of telegraph poles, transferring information
along with the electrical impulses that make the telegraphs
work. Iron Riders find these creatures very effective means of
passing information to others of their tribe, and also use them
to learn what is being said to whom by means of the telegraph.
Willpower 2, Rage 1, Gnosis 3, Power 10
Charms: Airt Sense, Solidify Reality

Banes

Banes, the spirit minions of the Wyrm, are beyond redemp-
tion and filled with the Corrupter's foul venoms. Banes assume
countless shapes, for destruction isn't limited to any single form.
Certain Banes are avatars or personifications of principles such
as hate or disease; others are simply random demonic entities.
There is little doubt that Banes are the Garou's greatest enemies.

All Banes are lost to the Wyrm, and the best way to fight these
vile spirits and ensure the removal of their influence is typically to
destroy them. Even their obliteration is no guarantee that the
damage they've caused can be undone, for their dark emotions are

gifts of the Wyrm, and often spread like plagues to any humans or
sentient creatures in the area. These emotions fester, growing
stronger until a Blight forms in the Umbra near a certain area.
Blights draw other Banes to the location, allowing them to feed on
the hatred and bitterness of the infected people. In this way, the
Wyrm's influence grows like an unchecked cancer. Certain rites of
cleansing and healing are often an effective way to undo and outdo
the Banes' works of corruption, but all too many werewolves claim
that the expediency of violence is the only tenable solution.

When in their true form, Banes are often revolting in
appearance, causing an effect rather like the Delirium in
humans. Few who've seen a Bane manifest are quite the same
again, and most suffer nightmares for the rest of their lives.

Most Banes have the Possession Charm, and many have
specific powers based around the emotion or force they charac-
terize. These powers can range from basic emotion control or
the talent to remove the effects of the Delirium — allowing
humans to see Garou for what they really are — to the ability
to spread living corruption throughout the reality of a Realm.

Storytellers are encouraged to let their imaginations run
wild when creating Banes, so long as their powers reflect the
forces they represent. These powers often require "
which can be accomplished through the expenditure of Power

278

ctivation,"

or by making successful Gnosis rolls. Many Banes have powers
of corruption or mutation, and Garou who fight these creatures
risk their bodies and souls.

The Storyteller should avoid letting Banes take the blame for
every form of human evil. Banessimply magnify and feed on the evil
inherent in a person's soul; they aren't readily attracted to anyone
who isn'tready tocome to the Wyrm. Banes tend tochoose the path
of least resistance, focusing on the deranged or corrupted instead of
first creating the darkness thatallows them toenterasoul. Ofcourse,
no one is fully immune to their corrosive touch — but such is life.

The Banes listed here are but a few of the many Wyrm-spirits
that infect the Tellurian. Storytellers should feel free to create
their own Banes as well, using those given here as guidelines.

Wyrm-Winds

Wyrm-Winds are what remains of air elementals and Desert
Windsthathave been corrupted by the Wyrm. These powerful Banes
resemble very strong dust devils when materialized, throwing sandsat
speeds of up to 100 miles per hourand uprooting plants wherever they
go. While in the material world, these Banes are capable of
creatingsandstorms that coverentire regions. Inthe Umbra,
they look more like a thunderhead in the shape ofa rotted
skull. Most Garou believe that Wyrm-Winds are created @

Werewolf: The Wild West


by thepollutantsspilling into the air near mining townsandalong the
tracks of the railroads; the Uktena believe these Banes are the
offspring of the Storm Eater. Wyrm-Winds are malevolent and
delight in spreading their corruption to others. Where these Banes go
unchecked, Blightssoon form. Worst ofall, Wyrm-Windsare starting
to become a regular occurrence in the Savage West.

Willpower 8, Rage 10, Gnosis 4, Power 55

Charms: Airt Sense, Blighted Touch, Corruption, Create
Wind, Disorient, Materialize, Reform, Updraft

Mind Eaters

The Mind Eaters were once Weaver-spirits, but the Storm
Eater changed all that. When encountered in the Umbra,
these Weaver-Banes appear as massive spiderlike creatures
with blackened hides and seeping wounds. When manifested
in the Gaia-Realm, they are surprisingly small, ticklike arach-
nids that immediately burrow into the skulls of anyone they
encounter. Normally more of these Banes are seen in the
physical world than in the Umbra; the Uktena believe that the

Mind Eaters actually break into smaller entities when

manifested. These powerful Banes manifest in areas

where people are numerous and where towns of any
size have popped up.

Night Hunters

Night Hunters are insidious Banes that possess individuals who
havea greedy nature. These spirits are permanently materialized in
the Gaia-Realm and, if the Uktena are correct, were once held
imprisoned in the forms they now possess. Each Night Hunter is
different in shape, but all of them appear as a small stone carving on
a leather thong when encountered. To people of a particularly
greedy nature, these stones appear to be made of gold or gems; when
they're spotted, the Hunters call out to their victims, seducing them.
with promises of wealth. Night Hunters have been the objects of
pistol duels, knife fights and even riots, as numerous individuals
have fought for the possession of the "precious" uinkets. Whoever
survives such an encounter becomes the new host for the Night
Hunter. Once possessed, the host continues on with his or her
normal life, never realizing that the treasure he wears (carefully
hidden on the body at all times) holds sway when the sun sets.

When night falls, the Bane takes possession of the body and
uses a Moon Bridge to join with the other Night Hunters. Asa
group, the 13 Night Hunters move through the desert, stalking
Garou as their main source of prey. They aren't too selective,
however, and will kill anything that crosses their collective path.

All of the Night Hunters carry pistols or rifles, all ride "horses"
that never tire. No barrier seems capable of stopping the Bane-
possessed souls once they've set out to accomplish a task; the Night
Huntersand their mountsare capable of moving through solid rock.
Atpresentonly four Night Hunters have manifested. Ifthe Uktena,
who continue to remain secretive about the exact origin of these
Banes, are correct, there are a total of 13 of the beasts.

Manifested Night Hunters
Physical: Strength 3, Dexterity 5, Stamina 4

Mental: Perception 4, Intelligence 4, Wits 4
Talents: Alertness 2, Athletics 3, Brawl 3, Dodge 4, Intimida-
tion 4
Skills: Animal Ken 2, Firearms 4, Leadership 3, Melee 4, Ride 4,
Stealth 3, Survival 5

(Spanish, French, German, at least one Native American),
Medicine 3, Occult 4

Gifts/Powers: All Night Hunters have Immunity to the De-
lirium, Eyes of the Wyrm and Ghost Walk (see below)

Gnosis 8, Willpower 9

tated

Image: The Night Hunters all appear in typical clothes,
varying only to the extent that the clothes often come from
different regions. There is only darkness where the faces of the
Night Hunters should be, save for eyes that glow asickly green.

Roleplaying Hints: Ask questions of the locals wherever
you may be. Find out about any unusual deaths in the area.

Never smile. Never laugh. Speak in a gravely voice, one
unaccustomed conversation. Kill any Garou you see, because
you remember the centuries you spent locked away all too well.
Serve the Storm Eater well, because it freed you.

Storyteller Notes: Ghost Walk allows a Night Hunter to
move through solid objects and effectively removes the crea-
ture from harm with a Gnosis roll (difficulty 5) and the
expenditure of 2 Power. When Ghost Walking, the Night
Hunter may not attack, nor may he suffer physical damage.

Skin Walkers

Skin Walkers are Wyrm-corrupt shamans capable of devastating
anareaina very short time. These Native American mysticsnormally
startoff with the bestof intentions, but theirangerat the mistreatment
of their people and their own curiosity about the spiritual world often
lead them to disaster. Once possessed by a Bane — the exact nature
of the Bane in question is unknown, a problem that worries most of
theGarou—theshaman becomesauniqueformofmockery, one that
seldom leaves populated areas alone for very long.

Skin Walkers seem to seed an area with new fomori, passing
new Banes to other people through the use of specially crafted
Bane-fetishes. Anyone unfortunate enough to encounter a
Skin Walker soon finds they can't remember what happened
after the chance meeting took place. Within a few days they
also discover that there are increasing lapses in their memories;
blank spots that only survivors of their assaults can account for
with any accuracy. There are very seldom any survivors to tell
the tale.

Physical: Strength 2, Dexterity 2, Stamina 2

Social: Charisma 2, Manipulation 2, Appearance 2

Mental: Perception 4, Intelligence 3, Wits 4

Talents: Intimidation 3, Subterfuge 2

Skills: Animal Ken 4, Firearms 4, Stealth 3, Survival 4
Knowledges: Enigmas 3, Investigation 3, Linguistics, Medi-
cine 2, Occult 4

Backgrounds: Resources 1

Powers: Corruption (as the Bane Charm), Eyes of the Wyrm,
Stone Seed (see below)

Gnosis 6, Willpower 7

Image: The Skin Walkers are universally very pale, with
dark hair and Native American attire. Their eyes are very dark,
with blood-red pupils that pulse independent of any light
source. Many of the Skin Walkers drip an oily sweat con-
stantly, and most bear bloodstains on their hands and around
their mouths.

Roleplaying Hints: Smile serenely and lift your blow
gun to your mouth. Never speak.

Werewolf: The Wild West


Storyteller Notes: Stone Seed allows a Skin Walker to use
a blow gun to infect a person with a Bane froma distance. The
Skin Walker must first successfully hit his victim (Firearms +
Dexterity, difficulty 6); the power also costs one Gnosis. A
Victim struck with one of these minuscule stones is likely to
feel no more pain than a mosquito bite, and seldom realizes
that he's been attacked. Once the stone is in the body, it
dissolves immediately, releasing the Bane that sleeps inside.

These Banes, called Debauchers, immediately instill
within a host a need to hurt others in a ritualistic manner.
In short, the host becomes a serial killer. After a few weeks
of struggling against these violent urges, the victim (if too
weak to resist the temptations) becomes a full-blown fomor.
The exact nature of the resulting mockery is left to the
Storyteller's discretion.

Debauchers

Willpower 7, Rage 10, Gnosis 8, Power 20 (+ 10 per person
killed by host)

Charms: Airt Sense, Materialize, Possession

Texas Tarantulas

They say everything grows bigger in Texas. In the case of
certain spiders, that statement seems to be true. The Texas
Tarantul:
were first spotted in the area around Nacogodoches, Texas.
Ranging in size between three feet and seven feet in height,
these creatures are terrifying and voracious. Worst of all, they
tend to run in packs. The Tarantulas were first spotted only a
few weeks after the first appearance of the Storm Eater; most
Garou blame the powerful Bane for their existence.

re actually found everywhere in the West, but

The statistics below depict a "small" Texas Tarantula,
averaging three feet in height with a body length of almost five
feet. Bigger critters are tougher accordingly.

Physical: Strength 3, Dexterity 5, Stamina 3

Social: all 0

Mental: Perception 4, Intelligence 1, Wits 2

Talents: Alertness 3, Athletics 3, Brawl 3, Dodge 4

Skills: Stealth 3, Survival 2

Powers: Extra Limbs, Fangs and Claws,
Poison Bite (see below)
Rage 4, Willpower 5
Health Levels: OK,
OK, OK, OK, -1,
-l, -1, -2, -2, -5,
Incapacitated

Image: Texas Tarantulas bear a strong resemblance to their
namesake, but only in general shape. These Wyrmlings have a
hard carapace, more like a crab's shell than a spider's exoskeleton,
and anywhere between eight and 30 small eyes on their heads. A
small number of these monsters even sport claws similar to those
of a crab; these claws are capable of inflicting aggravated damage.
The color of their carapace changes subtly from light gray to dark
reddish brown, allowing them to blend into the surrounding
landscape. Once moving, however, they're easy to spot.

Roleplaying Hints: Find food, kill it, eat it.

Storyteller Notes: The venom of Texas Tarantulas isn't
fatal, but a bitten victim must roll Stamina (difficulty 7) or
suffer paralysis. Once paralyzed, the victim is unable to react to
any physical stimuli, but feels everything. Due to their sheer
size and their ability to resist many natural poisons, Garou in
Crinos form require two injections of venom to fully succumb
to the paralysis. One dose, however, is sufficient to halve their
effective Dexterity.

Wyrm Clouds
A very new development in the ways of the Wyrm is the
Wyrm Clouds. These portals to the heavens seem to appear
only where the railroads run, and normally manifest at set
times; always in accordance with the train schedules, strangely
enough. The portals are swirling masses of black clouds lined
with flashes of green lighting. When they open, a stream of
Banes pours into the Gaia-Realm, heading directly for the train
below. Anyone on the train who is at risk of Wyrm-corruption
becomes a mockery; those few who are left alone by the Banes
soon become meals for the newly formed servants of the Wyrm.
As the phenomenon is very new, no Garou has yet discov-
ered what causes the Wyrm Clouds to form, or exactly where
in the Umbra they lead. Of all the Garou, only the Bone
Gnawers can attest to having actually seena Wyrm Cloud. The
portals opened during this event seldom last longer than five
minutes, and leave no trace once they've dissipated. The
precise timing of these things leads most Garou to believe that
the Clouds are guided by a mundane hand; maybe a human,
fomor, Black Spiral Dancer or even a cor-
rupt Iron Rider? Many Iron Rider
septs have already offered a
high fetish bounty tothe
pack that prevents
the WyrmClouds
from running
on time.


Below is a list of fetishes the Storyteller may use in a
chronicle. They are ranked from one to five, corresponding
with the Fetish Background Trait. Characters with this
Background may have one or more of these fetishes, up to
their level in the Background, or the Storyteller may
make up fetishes of his own.

Storytellers are encouraged to create their own
fetishes, using the basic rules outlined in Chapter
Seven (Fetishes, pg. 209). However, they should
avoid using too many of these items in their stories, as
they are rare and thus rightly prized.

The Gnosis rating indicated is the difficulty a Garou
must meet or exceed in a Gnosis roll in order to use the fetish
in question.

Medicine Bag

Level varies, Gnosis varies
Medicine Bags are fairly common among the native
Garou, and are used for a multitude of purposes. These can
include: Peace (Level 1, Gnosis 5), which increases the
difficulty of all Rage rolls by +3; Protection from Spirits
(Level 3, Gnosis 6), which prevents an uninvited
spirit from manifesting with 100 feet of the
bearer; Healing (Level 4, Gnosis 8),
which allows the bearer to heal at
twice the normal rate; and
Stealth (Level 2, Gnosis 4),
which reduces all Stealth-re-
lated difficulties by two.
The most powerful Medicine Bags (Level
5, Gnosis 8) allow a Garou to roam among
humans in any form and still appear
human (though violent acts are still
perceived normally), or fool Wyrm-
minions into perceiving the bearer as a
kindred spirit. Many Medicine Bags do
more than one of the above in con-
junction, but are always of
appropriately high level and require
very high Gnosis to activate. Appro-
priate spirits must be bound into
Medicine Bags, and all but the most
powerful abilities of the Medicine Bag
require concentration in order to op-
erate properly.

Medicine Bags are always created by
the elders of the Uktena and Wendigo
tribes, and are seldom awarded to any-
one from other tribes. These powerful

fetishes are usually attuned to the wearer
when they are presented.

Dream
Catcher

Level 2, Gnosis 4

The Dream Catcher isa simple hoop of wood, designed with
a web of sinew in its center. When activated, this fetish
captures and holds any Banes attempting to possess the owner.
This fetish is actually fairly common, but few Europeans know
how to make one work properly.

To create a Dream Catcher, a Protection, Guardian or
Weaver-spirit must be coerced into the sinew web.

Turtle Necklace

Level 3, Gnosis 7

One of the few remaining artifacts of the lost Croatan, this
turtle-shell necklace is a potent fetish of protection. When
activated, it grants its wearer an extra soak die against all
attacks, including silver, fora scene. The few Turtle Necklaces
were gifts from the Croatan to the other Pure Ones, and some
doubt that any will be left in a century or so. Presumably a
Turtle-spirit must be bound into a necklace to create this
fetish, but no other tribe has had any luck doing so.

Klaive

Level 4, Gnosis 6

A klaive is a ritual dagger of the European Garou. Klaives
are rare weapons, for they are made of silver and their manufac-
ture and use is deadly to the Garou. A klaive is about the size
of a Bowie knife, and a Garou who carries one loses one point
from his effective Gnosis Rating. A War-spirit is usually bound
into a klaive, allowing it to do aggravated damage to non-
Garou enemies. Without the spirit, the klaive only causes
aggravated damage to other shapechangers; however, a Garou
need not attune a klaive without a spirit.

Pulling a klaive on another Garou is considered a grave
action, for a klaive duel is almost always to the death.

The difficulty to attack with a klaive is 6, and it
inflicts Str +2 damage. Garou can't soak this damage
because it's a silver weapon,

Werewolf: The Wild West


Spirit Axe
Level 4, Gnosis 6
The Uktena fashion spirit axes to help them battle the

minions of the Wyrm. These weapons act as normal axes

when used against an enemy, save when their target is pos-
sessed by a Bane or spirit. In the latter event, the spirit axe
ignores the physical person, simply passing through solid flesh
as if it weren't there, and attacks the spirit instead.

To create a spirit axe, a Predator-spirit must be convinced
to join with the specially crafted weapon.

War Bow

Level 4, Gnosis 7

War bowsare intricately carved from bone and often adorned
with numerous feathers and beads. The:
are much like the klaives of the European Garou in that they

e powerful weapons

are usually passed down to descendants in the same family. The
war bow is a weapon first, and fires arrows at a -2 difficulty,
while inflicting Strength +2 aggravated damage.

To create a war bow, a War-spirit, and sometimes an
Ancestor-spirit, must be bound within the weapon.

Spirit Whistle

Level 4, Gnosis 8

When activated, this small bone whistle emits a powerful
shrieking noise that causes great pain to all spirits within the
wielder's line of sight. Any spirits present must roll Gnosis,

difficulty 6, and score equal to or greater than the user's successes

on the activation roll in order to avoid fleeing in pain and fear.
In order to make a Spirit Whistle, a Screech Owl-
spirit, or a spirit of Madness, Screaming or Discord
must be bound into the whistle.

Gaia's Breath

Gnosis 5

Gaia's Breath is a small
dried gourd marked with sym-
bols of praise to Gaia. When
crushed and spilled over an open
wound, the powdered gourd heals
up to four Health Levels, even if the

Bane Arrows

Gnosis 4

These obsidian-headed arrows are able to
home in on Banes, whether they are visible or
not. These arrows hit automatically and in-
flict three dice of aggravated damage. The bite
of a Bane arrow is so painful that no Bane can
resist crying out in pain.

To create a Bane arrow, one of the following
spirits must be bound into the arrow: War, Air or
Pain.

Vision Pool

Gnosis 6

A vision pool is a simple powder which, when
added to a clear pool of water, shows visions of the
future. These talens are used for divination, but are
often convey vague messages , requiring an Enig-
mas roll (difficulty 6) to comprehend.

To create a vision pool, a Divination-spirit
must be bound into the ashes of burned peyote.

Pack Totems

The process of allying with or "adopting" a
pack totem is more like that of a parent adopting
achild than two friends devoting themselves to
each other. The totem feels a tie to its adopted
pack and speaks to those Garou it chooses as its
"children." The actual spirit that attends to the
werewolf pack is a Jaggling representative called a
totem avatar.

Generally a totem spirit isan Incarna. Totem spirits
are usually animal spirits, but can be mythological crea-
tures, elemental forces or other kinds of spirits. Animal
totems are also called Animal Fathers by the lupus Garou.
Most totems fall into one of three categories: Totems of
Wisdom, Totems of Respect or Totems of War.

© Totems of Respect: These ancient spirits are associ-
ated with the greatest of Garou. They are totems of honor.
They have provided leadership advice, diplomatic ass
tance, and other kinds of aid for many years. They have been
known to lend their iron will to their children.

© Totems of War: These are most often predatory animal-
spirits and ancestral warriors. They are totems of Rage and
glory, aiding the Garou in battle, tactics, strategy and recon-
noitering. They are perhaps too bloodthirsty to win respect
from the Garou as a whole, but they are helpful in times of
battle. They have been known to lend Rage to their children,
and to help them in times of war.

© Totems of Wisdom: These are wild, spiritual totems,
usually nature-spirits and elemental forces. Though they teach
many werewolf mystics the secrets of magic, and they grant aid

Werewolf: The Wild West

OX)

R

to Theurges. these totems are often looked on with a measure
of disrespect because they are unpredictable and speak in

riddles. They have been known to teach their children Gifts
and to protect them spiritually.

Background Costs
and Traits

The Totem Background enables characters to "pur-
chase" a totem-spirit affiliated with their pack. The
total number of Background points allocated to the
totem by the characters to the pack totem provides the
pack with a pool of points to spend. However, the pack
must first spend the Background points for the totem the
members select, and then provide the totem with other
abilities. For more information, see Totem, pg. 137.
All Traits granted by a totem are on a per-turn basis,
and are granted to only one pack member at a time
(with the exception of Renown, unless otherwise
stated). Traits may be passed back and forth among
the pack members; the current power-wielder desig-
nates the next recipient of the Trait. All Attribute and
Ability bonuses add to the wielder's Dice Pool for that
Trait, even ifthe character doesn't normally have that
Trait. All Renown bonuses and penalties apply im-
mediately to the pack members' Renown as soon as
they have selected a totem.
Most totems have a Ban, a rule or rules that its
followers must follow in order to maintain the totem's
favor. Those who violate the Ban find themselves in
disfavor with their pack totem, and must undergo a Rite
of Contrition before the totem will aid them again. It's
possible for a totem to totally abandon a pack that
repeatedly violates its Ban.
Pack totems may teach new Gifts to those Garou it
finds worthy as with other spirits. The types of Gifts
they teach relate to their affiliations and powers.

Below are lists of available totems and their
affiliations. Many of these totems are also tribal
with a to-

totems. Members of a tribe allied
tem don't gain the

Traits listed with
each totem; these
bonuses only ap-
ply to those
packs allied to
an avatar of that
totem. Tribes
benefit from the °
wisdom and pa-
tronage of their
totem; their tribal

by their tribal totem.


|

Totems of Respect

Falcon

Background Cost: 5

Noble and brave, Falcon soars high and leads all Garou.
Falcon helps werewolves be strong through his insight into
their hearts. The Silver Fangs serve Falcon, who helps them
maintain communication and unity. Falcon is a highly
respected totem.

Traits: Packs chosen by Falcon may call upon four Will-
power points per story, and gain +3 to a Leadership Dice Pool.
Each pack member's Honor rating increases by one temporary
point. Silver Fangs often look upon the Children of Falcon as
nobles, though some may scoff and consider them usurpers.

Ban: Children of Falcon can never allow themselves to lose
permanent Honor. If they do, they are expected to right this
misdeed or perform the Rite of Contrition and atone by
throwing themselves into an impossible battle with a minion
of the Wyrm, thus committing suicide.

Grandfather Thunder

Background Cost: 7

Thunder is more feared than respected, yet his place
among the Totems of Respect is secure. His Stormcrows have
eyes everywhere, and he rarely chooses a pack on his own.
Instead of relegating a totem avatar to a pack, Grandfather
Thunder sends one of his Stormcrows to tend his children.
Grandfather Thunder teaches patience, ingenuity, subtlety
and strategy to those he chooses.

Traits: Packs chosen by Thunder can call upon an extra
five Willpower points per story and gain +3 to Etiquette Dice
Pools. Very few people trust them, but Grandfather Thunder's
children gain +2 to Intimidation Dice Pools by invoking
Thunder. Each pack member gains one temporary Honor,
and Shadow Lords watch the pack closely.

Ban: Children of Thunder don't usually tell the truth to those
they do not respect. They never respect anyone they can dominate.

Pegasus

Background Cost: 4

Pegasus is known as the tribal totem of the Black Furies, but
also for her nobility and power. Pegasus is chiefly concerned
with sacred places and their protection. Because of the ongoing
feud between the Black Furies and the Get of Fenris, the totem
will accept no pack with a Get as a member. She teaches her
children Gifts associated with the air.

Traits: Pack members gain an extra three Willpower points
perstory and +3 to Animal Ken Dice Pools. Each member gains
+2 temporary Honor Renown. Black Furies will always be well-
disposed toward the Children of Pegasus.

Ban: The Children of Pegasus must always give aid to
females of all species, especially the young. Naturally,
this doesn't include any females with a Wyrm-taint.

Stag

Background Cost: 4

Stag is the master of the Wild Hunt, a representation of the
wild, masculine power of nature. He isan ancient totem, farolder
than the Garou, and represents one of the fundamental aspects
of life. He taught the Garou their affinity with nature and was,
perhaps, instrumental in the ending of the Impergium. He isalso
associated with virility. Stag will occasionally appear to lost
Garou, leading them out of danger or aiding them in a crisis.

Traits: Packs chosen by Stag have three extra Willpower
per story, and gain +3 Survival. Each pack member gains three
temporary Honor Renown. The Fianna are well-disposed to
the followers of Stag, as are European faeries.

Ban: Children of Stag must always aid the fae and their kin.

Totems of War

Bear

Background Cost: 5

Great Bear isa fierce warrior, but very wise in peacetime. He is an
ancient healer and ritemaster, old and wise in the ways of the Gaia-
Realm. He is viewed unfavorably by the European Garou, however,
because they distrust his true children, the Gurahl (werebears).

Traits: Children of the Bear gain +3 to Medicine Dice Pools
and the ability to hibernate for up to three months with no
need for food or water. Each member's Strength Attribute is
permanently raised by one, and each pack member may use the
Gift: Mother's Touch once per day.

Unfortunatly, all members of the pack also lose five tempo-
rary Honor Renown, and subtract one from all earned temporary
Honor Renown when rewarded. Werebears look favorably
upon the Children of Bear, as do most native people.

Ban: Bearasks nothing of his children, save that they not attack the
Gurahl without reason. He knows they give much in service to him.

Coyote

Background Cost: 7

Coyote is the Trickster. He is wild, unpredictable and often an
outlaw. He is the shadow in the woods, the root that trips even the
mightiest wolf. Coyote's howl rises up to Luna and calls Her down to
dance. He is a great warrior, a master of deception and skullduggery.

Traits: Coyote gives his children +3 Larceny, +3 Stealth,
+1 Subterfuge and +1 Survival. He can always find his chil-
dren, wherever they are. The Nuwisha werecoyotesare inclined
to play tricks less likely to kill on Coyote's children.

However, the Garou do not see Coyote as an especially wise
totem. Each pack member subtracts one from any temporary
Wisdom Renown awards they receive. The pack is constantly
blamed for troubles, whether or not it's at fault. Coyote isa very
powerful totem, but difficult to follow. Still, boredém is never
a problem for the Children of Coyote.

Ban: Coyote asks only that his children follow their hearts
and avoid the Wyrm's embrace.


@4

Fenris

Background Cost: 5

Great Fenris treads the world in a haze of Rage. He is the feral
Dire Wolf, the destroyer of all that is weak. Fenris is bloodthirsty
and powerful, giving no quarter and expecting none in return.

Traits: Fenris grants one additional Physical Attribute pointto
the pack; the current holder of this Trait can choose if it goes to
Strength, Dexterity or Stamina, even if the additional point
increases the Attribute over 5. Each pack member gains two
temporary Glory Renown. The Get of Fenris respect those who
follow Fenris more than they do most others, and will constantly
invite the Children of Fenris to join them on wild, frenzied hunts.

Ban: Fenris demands that no child of his ever refuse a
chance to fight.

Griffin

Background Cost: 4

The ever-hungry Griffin murders with lightning claws and feeds on.
his fallen enemies. He guards the wildemess against human encroach-
ment and represents the most primal and animalistic powers.

Traits: Griffin grants +3 Alertness to his vigilant children.
Additionally, each pack member can communicate directly
with any bird of prey, without the need of a special Gift. Red
Talons recognize the Children of Griffin as kindred souls, even
those few homids who follow him.

Ban: Griffin requires that his children not associate with
humans. He demands that they hunt humans foolish enough to
stray into his domains. Only the most exceptional homid Garou
are accepted by Griffin.

Rat

Background Cost: 5
Silent, quick and crafty, Rat is a master of guerrilla warfare,
survival and camouflage. Rat finds safety innumbers, and has taught
many Garou the virtues of his wisdom. Although not that belliger-
eat, Ratisevery inch thescrapper, and quite violentwhencomered.
Traits: Rat grants his children five Willpower points per story,
+ ad teaches them how best to fight a foe. All pack members
subtract one from all difficulties involving a bite maneuver, and
‘om all attempts at stealth. Each pack member gains one point of
"emporary Wisdom Renown. Bone Gnawers aid Children of Rat
when they can, but won't generally risk their own lives to do so.
Ban: Rat asks that his children never kill vermin.

Wendigo

Background Cost: 7

Wendigo is the great cannibal spirit, devouring the hearts of
<s foes and howling its rage to the world. Wendigo is far too
uuch of the Wyld to be tainted by the Wyrm, and is said to have
z hatred for the Corrupter that is even hotter than its own frozen
Lide is cold. Wendigo teaches the Garou how to use the wild
tactics of the storm in battle. It also teaches them to use the cold
bitterness within them, converting it into a burning rage.

Werewolf: The Wild West
BR

Traits: Each pack member gains five Rage points per story,
regardless of the actual Rage rating of each; thus, the Children of
Wendigo may sometimes have more Rage to spend than their
ratings allow. Each member also gains +2 temporary Glory Re-
nown. The Wendigo tribe doesn'tautomatically trust the Children
of Wendigo, for they acknowledge that the spirit of the frozen
wastes is often too wild and unpredictable for sound judgment.

Ban: Wendigo asks that its children help native people
when they are in trouble.

Totems of Wisdom
Buffalo

Background Cost: 6

Buffalo is sturdy, patient, slow to anger and filled with the
wisdom of the future. His dreams of prophecy and words of
wisdom have stopped wars before they could start.

Traits: Buffalo grants +3 Stamina to the pack's soak pool,
and subtracts four from all difficulties in interpreting dreams.
His children also gain +3 Survival when searching for food and
safe water. Uktena and Wendigo Garou consider followers of
Buffalo friends, unless the pack proves otherwise.

Ban: Buffalo asks that his children protect the great herds
of buffalo that roam the ranges. This Ban doesn't mean pre-
venting the death of a buffalo for food, but rather is a request
todefend the herd from those who would slaughter them by the
hundreds for profit.

Chimera

Background Cost: 5

Chimera is She of Many Faces, the Lady of Mirrors, the
Ever-Changing. She is the mysterious caretaker of dreams, and
is thus the mistress of enigmas and inner wisdom.

Traits: Chimera gives her children the ability to disguise
themselv: something or someone else while in the Umbra.
The pack gains +3 to Enigmas Dice Pools and +1 to Perception.
Each pack member subtracts two from all difficulties involving
solving puzzles, enigmas, finding things or interpreting dreams.
Each pack member also gains two temporary points of Wisdom.
Stargazers will note the pack's affiliation, but this won't neces-
sarily affect their opinion of the pack.

Ban: Chimera doesn't limit her children, but won't visit
them if they don't seek enlightenment.

Cockroach

Background Cost: 6

Cockroach is old and cunning, the master of adaptation. The
Iron Riders revere Cockroach, and in turn it aids them in
learning to survive in the rapidly changing world. Though
incredibly ancient, Cockroach is a totem of the modern age.
Whatever works, its children inevitably shrug.

Traits: Cockroach's children gain the ability to
Peek from the Umbra and hear the words communi-
cated by telegraph, simply by listening. They may also


communicate to
one another simply by
speaking into the wires that
lead from one telegraph station to the
next. Followers of Cockroach also re-
duce the difficulty toaffectany technology
by two; this includes any Crafts rolls.
Ban: Cockroach asks that all of its kin be
spared by its packs.

Rattlesnake

Background Cost: 8
Rattlesnake is an old, wise totem of the Pure
Ones. He is capable of great compassion and
even greater fury. When angered, Rattlesnake is
a harsh foe with a very long memory. Like his
namesake, Rattlesnake is easily offended. But
Rattlesnake's anger is almost ironic when one
considers his patience; he is capable of great things, and
demands that his children carefully consider any situ-
ation before acting. Once hisfollowersstrike, however,
there is seldom an enemy left alive.

Traits: Rattlesnake grants his children second
sight, giving them the Gift: Pulse of the Invisible. His
packs gain the ability to entrance listeners through
singing or howling (Manipulation + Performance,
difficulty 8); the actual effects are up to the Story-
teller. In addition, the pack gains Past Life 3; this is
not an actual affinity with Garou ancestors, but an
ability to learn ancient knowledge and lore by access-
ing primal memories. All Uktena and Wendigo will
recognize and honor Children of Rattlesnake.

Ban: Rattlesnake asks that none of his followersattack
any serpent, and that his children honor the Pure Ones.

Uktena

Background Cost: 6

Uktena is a dark, powerful and ancient water-
spirit with the features of a cougar and a serpent.
Few spirits know and understand the secrets of the
occult as well as Uktena.

Traits: Uktena

surrounds its children with a pro-
tective ward when they are in the
Umbra; this adds three dice to their soak
rolls. Each Child of Uktena is taught se-
cret lore and knowledge, gaining two extra
experience points per story that may only be
applied to learning Occult, Rituals, Gifts or
other mystical knowledge.

The pack's association with Uktena is dubi-
ous to other Garou; Social roll difficulties are
increased by one when dealing with other
werewolves (except the Uktena). Each pack
member gains two points of temporary Wisdom.
Garou of the Uktena will treat the packmates as
lost siblings.

Ban: Uktena asks that its children recover lost
mystical lore, places and animals from the Wyrm.

Unicorn

Background Cost: 7

Unicorn is pure, powerful, strong and She isa
totem of peace, harmony and healing. She represents
the pure understanding and unbridled love of Gaia,
and she extends this bliss to her children.

Traits: Unicorn's children gain her speed, mov-
ing at twice the normal rate when in the Umbra.
Followers of Unicorn subtract two from all difficul-
ties involving empathy and healing, but add two to
all difficulties involving harm and injury to other
Garou, excluding the Wyrm-tainted. They also gain
three additional dice when using Gifts involving
healing, peace, strength and protection. Each pack
member gains +3 temporary Wisdom Renown. Chil-
dren of Gaia will always give aid to and usually side
with the Children of Unicorn.

Ban: Unicorn asks that her children continu-
ally aid and protect the weak, the helpless, the
abused and the exploited, but not to the extent of
aiding the Wyrm: Unicom may be'a pacifist; but
she's no fool.
